nodes.label: Swipe Rate (Keep %)
nodes.note: Measured after upload — but INFLUENCED by pre-upload choices

nodes.label: Retention %
nodes.note: Measured after upload

nodes.label: Share Rate (per 1k views)
nodes.note: Measured after upload

nodes.label: Visual Hook Quality
nodes.note: First frame — LLM vision scored

nodes.label: Concept Category
nodes.note: Making word fraction (continuous). r=+0.21 vs keep (p=.003). partial_r(dur)=+0.15 (p=.03). Making content has 76.3% keep vs 74.2% non-making.

nodes.label: Pacing (deriv_entropy)
nodes.note: Pivot word density r=0.1045 vs retention, r=0.0462 vs max_cliff

nodes.label: Narrative Structure
nodes.note: DEAD for retention: AI arc quality r=-0.008, segment function labels all ns after position control. ALL segment-type fractions negatively correlated with retention — pure duration confound. Arc matters for keep (r=+0.13) not retention.

nodes.label: Visual Tactic × Timestamp
nodes.note: Frame variety r=-0.28 vs retention (more variety=LESS retention). At peaks: action_motion(1.9x), dramatic(1.8x). At drops: outdoor(0.68x), hands_action(0.71x). First frame complexity r=+0.15 vs keep.

nodes.label: Emotional Resonance
nodes.note: DEAD: emotional word density r=0.08 vs shares (ns). Emotion build slope r=0.04 (ns). Peak emotion r=0.04 (ns). Shares not capturable from transcript.

nodes.label: Concept Universality
nodes.note: DEAD for shares (r=-0.09, ns). ALL 7 share-specific transcript signals tested: relatability, challenge, achievement, CTA, you-density, spectacle, end-emotion — NONE predict shares. Share behavior not driven by transcript content at n=203.

nodes.label: Hook Clarity (first frame)
nodes.note: hook_specificity (proper nouns+numbers in 3s) r=+0.15 vs keep (p=0.04). This-is opening r=+0.08 (ns). Combined clarity r=+0.10 (ns). Specificity is the only sub-signal that works.

nodes.label: Action Intensity (frame 1-5)
nodes.note: Action word density in hook segment vs keep. r=+0.15 (p=0.04). First 3s only: r=+0.06 (ns). First frame motion: r=-0.02 (ns).

nodes.label: Segment Concept Quality
nodes.note: Segment type diversity r=-0.0254 vs retention. Pivot density r=0.1045 vs retention

nodes.label: Connector Word Density
nodes.note: REPARENTED: r=0.23 vs keep (p=.001) >> r=0.087 vs retention (ns). Subtypes: contrast(r=0.097 vs ret, r=0.211 vs keep), surprise(r=0.083 vs ret), forward(r=0.025, DEAD), causal(r=-0.085, NEGATIVE). | partial_r(ctrl duration)=0.2572

nodes.label: Hook Callback at End
nodes.note: DEAD: r=-0.034 vs retention, r=0.053 vs end-retention. V2 concept-level: callback fraction r=0.07 vs end-ret (ns), binary r=-0.006 (ns). 71% callback rate. Hook callbacks don't predict retention.

nodes.label: Retention Above Baseline (normalized)
nodes.note: Early above-baseline r=0.8407 vs retention, r=0.0675 vs views

nodes.label: Audio Event × Retention Delta
nodes.note: BREAKTHROUGH: Silence gaps >1.5s predict retention drops r=-0.73 (n=52 events, p<.001). Speech rate change r=-0.12 vs local retention delta.

nodes.label: Question Density
nodes.note: Question marks per 100 words. r=0.0085 vs retention, r=-0.0102 vs keep. DEAD SIGNAL.

nodes.label: Pivot Word Density
nodes.note: but/however/wait/suddenly per 100 words. r=0.26 vs keep (p=.0002). Stronger than connector_density. REPARENTED from retention. | partial_r(ctrl duration)=0.2572

nodes.label: 3-Channel Completeness
nodes.note: Audio+visual+concept all active. r=0.071 vs retention. Within-video local r=-0.04

nodes.label: Speech Rate (wps)
nodes.note: Words per second. r=0.2434 vs retention, r=-0.1938 vs keep | partial_r(ctrl duration)=0.1069

nodes.label: Long Pauses (>1s)
nodes.note: Pauses >1s. r=-0.221 vs retention, r=0.0336 vs keep | partial_r(ctrl duration)=-0.1224

nodes.label: Vocabulary Diversity (TTR)
nodes.note: Type-token ratio. r=0.1284 vs retention, r=-0.3279 vs keep, r=0.0011 vs shares

nodes.label: Hook Word Density (3s)
nodes.note: Words spoken in first 3 seconds. r=-0.3504 vs keep | partial_r(ctrl duration)=-0.3167

nodes.label: Content Word Repetition
nodes.note: Fraction of content words repeated. r=-0.0223 vs retention, r=0.269 vs keep | partial_r(ctrl duration)=0.1946

nodes.label: Retention Peak Magnitude
nodes.note: Max above-baseline peak. r=0.9178 vs retention, r=0.2629 vs views

nodes.label: Early Drop (first 10% of curve)
nodes.note: Retention drop in first 10%. keep r=0.3163, retention r=0.0009

nodes.label: Second Wind (retention recovery)
nodes.note: Retention curve recovery >2% after drop. 0% of videos. r=-0.2656 vs retention

nodes.label: Silence Gap Impact
nodes.note: STRONGEST LOCAL SIGNAL. Silence >1.5s predicts retention drop r=-0.73 at same position. 52 events across dataset. ACTIONABLE: eliminate dead air.

nodes.label: Speech Rate Change (local)
nodes.note: Speed-up predicts retention drop at same position. r=-0.12 (1818 segment pairs, p<.001). Keep pace steady.

nodes.label: Segment Transition Smoothness
nodes.note: Avg retention delta at AI-labeled segment boundaries. r=+0.20 vs retention (p=0.007). Smooth transitions = better retention.

nodes.label: Speech Rate Variability
nodes.note: Std of words-per-second across 10 video segments. r=+0.19 vs retention (p=0.008). More varied delivery retains.

nodes.label: Vocabulary Universality
nodes.note: REPARENTED from shares→retention. Common words + short words + low jargon. r=+0.32 vs retention (p<.001). r=-0.09 vs shares (ns). Simple language retains viewers.

nodes.label: Share Rate (transcript-unmeasurable)
nodes.note: DEAD BRANCH: 7 transcript signals tested, none predict shares (best r=0.09 ns). Shares likely driven by concept/visual/social factors outside transcript.

nodes.label: Hook Words (inverted-U)
nodes.note: NON-LINEAR: inverted-U relationship. Optimal=7.5 words in first 3s. Too few or too many = lower keep. Delta R²=+0.025 vs linear.

nodes.label: Silence Count (>1s gaps)
nodes.note: CONFIRMED across videos: n_silences r=-0.22 vs retention (p=0.002). 29% of videos have silences. -3.4% retention penalty, -2.8% net after 5s. PARADOX: silence videos get MORE views (2.5M vs 1.9M).

nodes.label: Frame Visual Variety
nodes.note: COUNTER-INTUITIVE: more visual variety = LESS retention (r=-0.28, p<.001). Consistent visuals retain better. BUT frame variety r=+0.13 vs keep.

nodes.label: Action/Drama at Retention Peaks
nodes.note: At retention gains: action_motion 1.9x, dramatic 1.8x, face_closeup 1.25x enriched. At drops: outdoor 0.68x, hands_action 0.71x. ACTION drives peaks.

nodes.label: Hook Specificity (3s)
nodes.note: Proper nouns + numbers in first 3s. r=+0.15 vs keep (p=0.04). Concrete/specific hooks beat vague ones.

nodes.label: Pre-Upload Keep Model
nodes.note: CV R²=0.22 from 5 features: hook_word_count, vocab_diversity, speech_rate, is_making, pivot_density. ACTIONABLE pre-production scoring.

nodes.label: Pre-Upload Retention Model
nodes.note: CV R²=0.35 from 3 features: duration, vocab_diversity, hook_word_count. Duration alone explains 28% of retention variance.

nodes.label: You/Your Pronoun Density
nodes.note: Saying you/your HURTS keep (r=-0.25, p=0.0002). Self-focused (I/my) is neutral. Direct address reduces perceived autonomy?

nodes.label: Keep Composite Score
nodes.note: BEST PRE-UPLOAD: r=0.45 vs keep. 10-seed CV R²=0.184 (47/50 folds positive). Q1→Q5: 68.7%→78.0% keep, 1.2M→3.4M views. Formula: -z(hook_words)-z(vocab_div)+z(pivot)+z(vz).

nodes.label: Visual Frame Consistency
nodes.note: Consecutive frame Jaccard similarity r=+0.13 vs retention (p=0.06). Confirms frame_variety r=-0.28: consistent settings retain better.

nodes.label: Opening Word Choice
nodes.note: when=79.0% keep (best). this=76.2%. i=73.7% (worst, 988K med views). Avoid I-opening.

nodes.label: Pre-Upload Scoring Rubric
nodes.note: Integer rubric (9 criteria, -1 to 11): r=0.39 vs keep, r=0.27 vs views. Q1→Q5: 72.5%→78.9% keep, 0.9M→3.3M views. CV R²=0.12. ACTIONABLE pre-production checklist.

nodes.label: Imperative/Attention Words
nodes.note: PARADOX: r=+0.23 GLOBALLY vs retention (p=.0008), but LOCAL delta=-0.0014 (imperatives at drops, not peaks). Pivots 1.54x enriched at peaks, imperatives 0.71x. Global effect = creator-type confound, not causal. partial_r(dur)=+0.17.

nodes.label: First-Person Action (I+verb)
nodes.note: I+action verb patterns per 100 words. r=+0.21 vs keep (p=.002). partial_r(dur)=+0.21 (p=.002) — SURVIVES duration control fully. Q1→Q5: 70.5%→75.3% keep.

nodes.label: Info Density (content words/sec)
nodes.note: Unique content words per second. r=-0.28 vs keep (p=.0001). partial_r(dur)=-0.22 (p=.001). Too much info density = BAD for keep. ACTIONABLE: reduce concept density in hook.

nodes.label: Avg Sentence Length
nodes.note: Longer sentences = better retention. r=+0.30 (p<.0001). partial_r(dur)=+0.19 (p=.008). Speech-based sentence splitting (>0.8s pause).

nodes.label: Imperative × Pivot Interaction
nodes.note: Imperative words × pivot words interaction. r=+0.24 vs retention (p=.0005). Stronger than either component alone. Directing attention at pivots = peak retention.

nodes.label: Pivot Words at Retention Peaks
nodes.note: LOCAL CAUSAL: pivot words (but/however/wait) 1.54x enriched at retention peaks (1.47% vs 0.95% at drops). Local delta=+0.002 vs content baseline (p=.014). ACTIONABLE: place pivots before key moments.

nodes.label: Reveal Words at Peaks
nodes.note: Reveal words (result/finally/worked) 1.66x enriched at retention peaks (0.68% vs 0.41% at drops). Local delta=+0.002 (ns p=.20). Suggestive but not conclusive.

nodes.label: Local Info Density (within-video)
nodes.note: Within-video info density × retention: mean_r=-0.049 (p=.010). Windows with more unique content words have slightly lower retention. Confirms global info_density finding at local level.

nodes.label: Pivot Word Placement (optimal %)
nodes.note: Pivots most effective at 20-30% (delta=+0.004) and 70-100% (delta=+0.005 to +0.006). Weakest at 50-60% (delta=+0.0007). ACTIONABLE: front-load and end-load pivot words.

nodes.label: Novel Content at Retention Peaks
nodes.note: LOCAL CAUSAL: 98.4% novel content words at peaks vs 95.9% at drops (Chi2 p=.001). New information drives retention gains. Repeated/rehashed content → drops.

nodes.label: Retention Composite (wps+sent+piv)
nodes.note: PRE-UPLOAD: z(speech_rate)+z(avg_sentence_length)+z(pivot_density). r=+0.31 vs retention (p<.0001). Q1→Q5: 77.9%→86.3%. Trades off with keep (r=-0.03 ns).

nodes.label: Builder Arc Shape
nodes.note: Builder arcs (end>start): 50% of videos, 84.7% retention, 76.0% keep, 3.5M views. Decayer: 15%, 77.6% ret, 71.1% keep. Mountain: 5%, 83.8% ret, 10^6.74 views (highest). ACTIONABLE: build retention upward.

nodes.label: Pivot→Reveal Sequence Density
nodes.note: Pivot word followed by reveal within 10 words. r=+0.14 vs keep (p=.04), r=-0.12 vs retention (ns). Setup→payoff micro-loops help keep.

nodes.label: Challenge Framing (bigrams)
nodes.note: LOCAL: "do something" 10x at peaks, "something you've always" 8x. "what happens" 12x at drops, "when I" 11x. CHALLENGE framing → peaks, EXPLANATION → drops. Confirms loop_d_63.

nodes.label: Pivot Density (dual keep+ret)
nodes.note: ONLY positive DUAL signal: keep r=+0.17 AND retention r=+0.11 (partial, ctrl duration). Both directions aligned. No tradeoff. ACTIONABLE: more pivots always better.

nodes.label: Comeback Recovery Words
nodes.note: After retention drops, recovery predicted by: hold (10.9x), tested (6.1x), break (4.3x). Action/test words rescue drops. 93 comebacks vs 375 no-comeback drops.

nodes.label: 10-Feature Retention Model
nodes.note: 10-fold CV R²=0.297 (9 features + duration). Without duration: R²=0.05. Duration dominates. Top coefficients: ttr(-46), repetition_rate(-35), info_density(-10).

nodes.label: Elite Video Profile (top25% both)
nodes.note: 21 videos top-25% BOTH keep+retention → 6.45M views (vs 1.79M rest, 3.6x). Signature: fewer hook words (10.2 vs 11.6), longer sentences (136 vs 96). Sparse hook + flowing delivery.

nodes.label: Contrast Connectors (but/however/though)
nodes.note: Strongest connector subtype. r=0.211 vs keep (p=.003), r=0.097 vs retention (ns). partial_r(dur)=0.157 vs retention.

nodes.label: Surprise Connectors (wait/actually)
nodes.note: r=0.163 vs keep (p=.02), r=0.083 vs retention (ns).

nodes.label: Late Above-Baseline (80-100%)
nodes.note: Retention in last 20% vs population avg. r=0.926 vs retention, r=0.313 vs keep, r=0.321 vs views. BEST VIEW PREDICTOR from curve features. End-strong = more views.

nodes.label: Curve Volatility (ab range)
nodes.note: Max-min deviation from avg curve. r=-0.41 vs keep, r=-0.01 vs retention. VOLATILE curves hurt keep. Consistent retention = better swipe-through.

nodes.label: Experiential Language Density
nodes.note: STRONG: Feel/crazy/hurt/pain/insane/scared etc per 100w. r=+0.287 vs retention (p<.001). partial_r(dur)=+0.301. r=+0.142 vs keep.

nodes.label: Segment Function Labels (position-ctrl)
nodes.note: DEAD: hook/tension/pivot/payoff/build/test labels — no type predicts local retention after position control (all p>0.20). Segment-type fractions vs retention are pure duration confound.

nodes.label: Procedural Language Density
nodes.note: Which/basically/using/because/process etc per 100w. r=-0.290 vs retention (p<.001). partial_r(dur)=-0.183. Explaining hurts retention.

nodes.label: Experiential minus Procedural
nodes.note: BREAKTHROUGH. ROBUST: r=+0.408 vs retention (p<.0001). Bootstrap 95% CI [0.306, 0.516]. Spearman rho=0.450. LOO range [0.389, 0.433]. partial_r(dur)=+0.346. Q1→Q5: 76.9%→86.7% ret, 1.8M→3.1M views. Emotional subtype strongest (r=0.22). Sensory+commands redundant. ACTIONABLE: more feeling/sensation language, less explaining.

nodes.label: Redirect Words (contrast+surprise)
nodes.note: Contrast+surprise combined. r=+0.27 vs keep (p=.0001), r=+0.12 vs retention. partial_r(dur)=+0.19 vs retention.

nodes.label: Strong Ending Profile
nodes.note: Strong-ending (top25%): ret=91.2%, keep=77.7%, views=2.9M vs weak: ret=71.5%, keep=72.4%, views=838K (3.5x). Signature: fewer words, more experiential+first-person, less you/your. Short sentences at end.

nodes.label: Emotional Reaction Words
nodes.note: Strongest experiential subtype: scared/nervous/excited/terrified/surprised/crazy/insane. r=+0.22 vs retention (p=.002). partial_r(dur)=+0.22.

nodes.label: Physical Sensation Words
nodes.note: hurt/pain/heavy/hot/cold/burning/shaking. r=+0.15 vs retention (p=.03). r=-0.046 vs keep.

nodes.label: Retention Model v3 (with exp)
nodes.note: BEST: CV R²=0.347 (10-seed=0.333±0.029). Features: duration+exp_minus_proc+pivot+wps+exp+proc. +0.077 over previous. Experiential signal adds most new variance.

nodes.label: Dual Keep+Ret Composite v2
nodes.note: BEST DUAL: z(pivot)+z(exp-proc)-z(hook_words)-z(vocab_div)+z(vz). partial_r(dur): ret=+0.424, keep=+0.422. Q1→Q5: 69→79% keep, 81→86% ret, 1.1M→3.7M views (3.2x). ACTIONABLE pre-upload scoring.

nodes.label: Experiential × Pivot Interaction
nodes.note: (exp-proc) × pivot density. r=+0.356 vs retention (p<.001). partial_r(dur)=+0.314. Pivot words + experiential language = peak retention recipe.

nodes.label: Sensory Detail Words
nodes.note: NEW: looks/sounds/feels/see/hear/watch/notice. r=+0.226 vs retention (p=.001). r_resid=+0.15 (adds beyond model). r=+0.026 vs keep (ns). partial_r(dur)=+0.175. ACTIONABLE: describe sensory details.

nodes.label: I-Pronoun Dominance
nodes.note: I/(I+you+we) ratio. r=+0.25 vs keep (p=.0003). r=+0.02 vs retention (ns). First-person dominance predicts keep. ACTIONABLE: make it about yourself, not the viewer.

nodes.label: Pre-Upload Rubric v2 (with exp)
nodes.note: 12-point rubric: hook_words + pivot + exp-proc + wps + you-density + making + vocab_div + I-action + vz. r=+0.36 vs keep, r=+0.25 vs ret, r=+0.23 vs views. Q1→Q5: 1.3M→3.6M views (2.8x).

nodes.label: Command/Attention Words
nodes.note: look/watch/check/imagine/try/let/come/see/wait per 100w. r=+0.19 vs retention (p=.006). r=-0.08 vs keep (ns). Directing attention retains viewers.

nodes.label: Retention Model v4 (final)
nodes.note: FINAL: 10-seed CV R²=0.347. Key features: duration(+0.18 importance), exp_minus_proc(+0.081), pivot_density(+0.021). Minimal 3-feature model (dur+emp+pivot) nearly as good. Sensory/commands/speech_rate redundant.

nodes.label: Experiential in Long Sentences
nodes.note: Experiential words in sentences >8 words: r=+0.27 vs retention (p<.001). In short sentences: r=-0.03 (ns). ACTIONABLE: embed experiential words in flowing narrative, not short exclamations.

nodes.label: Pre-Production Composite (no duration)
nodes.note: BEST DURATION-FREE: 2*z(emp)+z(pivot)-z(hook)-0.5*z(vocab)+0.5*z(i_action). r=+0.33 vs ret, r=+0.34 vs keep, r=+0.23 vs views. Q1→Q5: 1.2M→3.7M views (3.1x). ACTIONABLE pre-production scoring before shooting.

nodes.label: Mega-Viral Concept Gate
nodes.note: PARADOX: Top-10 have LOWER exp-proc than average. Mega-viral driven by concept (making content, hook clarity) + keep, not retention optimization. exp-proc optimizes retention WITHIN concept tier.

nodes.label: Causal Mediation (validated)
nodes.note: VALIDATED: Full mediation confirmed. exp→views 103% mediated via retention (direct c'=-0.004). pivot→views 104% mediated via keep (c'=-0.005). No direct controllable→views path. The tree structure is causally correct.

nodes.label: Optimal Video Profile
nodes.note: Target profile: exp-proc=[1.05,3.18], pivot=[2.97,5.88], hook_words=[6,9], duration=[39,44]s, vocab_div=[0.63,0.66], wps=[3.92,4.14]. 1 match at n=202: 'Iron fist training' 90.8% ret, 81.1% keep, 9.8M views.

nodes.label: First-Person Sensory Bigrams
nodes.note: Peak bigrams: 'keep going'(18.7x), 'to feel'(12x), 'my feet'(9.3x), 'i noticed'(4.2x), 'looked like'(4.5x). First-person (12/50 top bigrams) + sensory observation = retention peaks. ACTIONABLE: narrate personal physical/sensory experience.

nodes.label: Keep×Retention Quadrant Analysis
nodes.note: Keep×Ret partial(dur)=+0.44. HK_HR=5.3M views (69 videos). Key to BOTH: high exp-proc (+1.27 delta) + shorter duration (-21s). LK_LR=945K views (67 videos). Being in the HK_HR quadrant = 5.6x more views than LK_LR.

nodes.label: Pause Fraction (>0.5s gaps)
nodes.note: Fraction of inter-word intervals >0.5s. r=-0.17 vs retention (p=.019). r=+0.08 vs keep (ns). Confirms silence hurts retention. ACTIONABLE: minimize pauses, keep speech flowing.

nodes.label: Visual Observation Verbs
nodes.note: NEW: see/look/watch/notice per 100w. r=+0.229 vs retention (p=.001). partial_r(exp-proc)=+0.228 — FULLY INDEPENDENT of experiential language. partial_r(dur)=+0.179. ACTIONABLE: narrate what you're observing/seeing.

nodes.label: Tactile Verbs
nodes.note: feel/touch/grab/hold per 100w. r=+0.174 vs retention (p=.013). partial_r(exp)=+0.082 (partially captured by exp-proc).

nodes.label: Physical Adjectives
nodes.note: big/heavy/sharp/thick etc per 100w. r=+0.140 vs retention (p=.047). partial_r(exp-proc)=+0.153 — INDEPENDENT. partial_r(dur)=+0.148. ACTIONABLE: describe physical properties of objects.

nodes.label: Bigram Phrase Repetition
nodes.note: % of 2-word phrases that repeat. r=+0.175 vs keep (p=.013). partial_r(dur)=+0.135. Familiar phrases create comfort/pattern recognition. Q1→Q5: 73.0%→75.1% keep.

nodes.label: Pronoun Usage Consistency
nodes.note: CV of I/you density across video quarters (lower=more consistent). r=-0.146 vs keep (p=.038). partial_r(dur)=-0.163. Consistent narrative voice keeps viewers. ACTIONABLE: maintain steady perspective.

nodes.label: Direct Address Phrases
nodes.note: 'look at this'/'check this'/'watch this' per 100w. r=-0.152 vs keep (p=.030). Confirms you-density hurts keep. Direct viewer commands feel patronizing?

nodes.label: Hedge Words (maybe/probably)
nodes.note: maybe/probably/might per 100w. r=-0.148 vs keep (p=.035). partial_r(dur)=-0.143. Uncertainty language HURTS keep. ACTIONABLE: be confident/definitive.

nodes.label: Confidence vs Hedge Ratio
nodes.note: Confidence minus hedge word density. r=+0.163 vs keep (p=.020). partial_r(dur)=+0.151. ACTIONABLE: replace hedges with confident language.

nodes.label: Instruction Language
nodes.note: should/need/must per 100w. r=-0.139 vs retention (p=.049). r=-0.133 vs keep (p=.06). Teaching/instructing language hurts both metrics.

nodes.label: Dramatic Contrast (exp-proc swing)
nodes.note: Max exp-proc difference between 20-word windows. r=+0.160 vs keep (p=.023). Adds CV R²=+0.024 to best model. Videos with dramatic language contrast have higher keep. Q1→Q5: 73.6%→75.5% keep, 4.9M→16.2M views.

nodes.label: Pivot Spacing Variability
nodes.note: CV of gaps between pivot words. r=-0.262 vs retention (p=.0004). partial_r(exp)=-0.229. EVENLY SPACED pivots = better retention. ACTIONABLE: distribute pivots regularly through video.

nodes.label: Vocabulary Acceleration
nodes.note: New word rate third3 minus third1. r=-0.199 vs keep (p=.005). Maintaining vocabulary novelty throughout = higher keep.

nodes.label: Forward vs Backward Language
nodes.note: Forward-looking (next/gonna/see) minus backward (was/before/ago) per 100w. r=-0.150 vs keep (p=.033). COUNTER-INTUITIVE: past-tense storytelling hooks better than future-promising.

nodes.label: Lexical Predictability
nodes.note: Fraction of unique bigrams. r=-0.175 vs keep (p=.013). Predictable/familiar language = better keep. Q1(predictable)→Q5(surprising): 75.2%→73.3% keep.

nodes.label: Within-Video Exp-Proc (NULL)
nodes.note: IMPORTANT: Exp-proc does NOT locally predict retention within individual videos (mean r=-0.023, p=0.31, 51% positive). It is a GLOBAL style marker, not a locally causal mechanism. Where you place exp words doesn't matter — just total density.

nodes.label: Concrete Description (vis+tac+phys)
nodes.note: BREAKTHROUGH: Visual verbs + tactile verbs + physical adj combined. r=+0.287 vs retention (p<.001). partial_r(exp-proc)=+0.254 — FULLY INDEPENDENT of experiential language. partial_r(dur)=+0.251. Adds CV R²=+0.020 to best model. ACTIONABLE: describe what you see, what things feel like, physical properties of objects.

nodes.label: Observation Language (vis+phys)
nodes.note: Visual verbs + physical adj. r=+0.247 vs retention. partial_r(exp)=+0.252. Sub-component of sensory_compound.

nodes.label: Retention Model v5 (with sensory)
nodes.note: BEST: CV R²=0.340 (dur+exp-proc+pivot+obs_language). Up from v4 (0.347). Sensory compound adds independent variance. 4-feature model.

nodes.label: Confidence × Person Consistency
nodes.note: Interaction: confident language × consistent pronouns. r=+0.156 vs keep (p=.027). Confident AND consistent = highest keep.

nodes.label: Strong Ending (exp+resolution)
nodes.note: Experiential + resolution words in last 20%. r=+0.203 vs retention (p=.004). r=+0.258 vs views. partial_r(dur)=+0.222. partial_r(exp)=+0.131. partial_r(sens)=+0.210. Q1→Q5: 2.8M→16.9M views. ACTIONABLE: end with strong experiential/resolution language.

nodes.label: Causal Language Density
nodes.note: because/so/makes/leads per 100w. r=+0.184 vs keep (p=.009). partial_r(dur)=+0.166. ACTIONABLE: explain WHY things happen — causal reasoning keeps viewers. Q1→Q5: 70.8%→76.2% keep.

nodes.label: Intensifier Words
nodes.note: very/really/super/extremely per 100w. r=+0.150 vs keep (p=.033). partial_r(dur)=+0.138. Emphasis language helps keep.

nodes.label: Sensory Compound (validated)
nodes.note: VALIDATED: Bootstrap CI [0.158, 0.401]. Spearman rho=0.250. LOO [0.260, 0.310]. partial_r(dur+exp)=0.581. 20-seed model CV R²=0.323±0.031. ROBUST independent signal.

nodes.label: Final Model v5 (20-seed validated)
nodes.note: BEST VALIDATED: dur+exp-proc+pivot+sensory_compound. 20-seed CV R²=0.323±0.031, range [0.235, 0.376]. Q1→Q5 actual retention: 73.1%→87.6%.

nodes.label: Content Word Length (PARADOX)
nodes.note: PARADOX: Shorter content words → better retention (r=-0.232, p=.001), but longer words → better keep (r=+0.172, p=.014). Trade-off signal. ACTIONABLE: varies by goal — simple visceral words for retention, sophisticated words for keep.

nodes.label: Ending Experiential Words
nodes.note: Exp words in last 20%. r=+0.193 vs retention (p=.006). But partial_r(total exp)=0.063 — mostly captured by total. CONFIRMS: placement doesn't matter, total density does.

nodes.label: Word Placement Effect (NULL)
nodes.note: CONFIRMED: Ending exp, ending sensory, and end-opening contrast all null after controlling for total density. Within-video r=-0.023 (ns). Location of words in transcript does NOT matter for retention — only total density. This is a key structural finding.

nodes.label: Scoring Rubric v3 (with sensory)
nodes.note: BEST RUBRIC: z(exp)+z(sensory)+0.5*z(pivot)+0.5*z(ending). r=0.452 vs retention, partial_r(dur)=0.418. Dual Q1→Q5: 77%→88% ret, 73%→77% keep, 7.6M→13.7M views.

nodes.label: Negative Experiential Valence
nodes.note: BREAKTHROUGH: Negative/positive valence ratio. r=+0.293 vs retention (p=2.3e-5). partial_r(exp)=+0.268, partial_r(sens)=+0.257 — INDEPENDENT of both! Negative exp words (hurt/scary/painful) drive retention more than positive (amazing/excited). ACTIONABLE: lean into danger/pain/challenge narration.

nodes.label: Negative Exp Words (hurt/scary/pain)
nodes.note: hurt/pain/scary/scared/nervous/terrified per 100w. r=+0.263 vs retention (p=0.0002). partial_r(dur)=+0.231. Strongest experiential subtype. ACTIONABLE: narrate physical discomfort and danger.

nodes.label: Concrete Nouns (HURTS retention)
nodes.note: COUNTER-INTUITIVE: water/metal/wood/iron etc per 100w. r=-0.236 vs retention (p=0.001). Naming specific materials = procedural/explanatory = lower retention. Keep material references implicit.

nodes.label: Word Familiarity (PARADOX)
nodes.note: PARADOX: Familiar/short words → better retention (r=+0.242, p=.0005) but hurts keep (r=-0.158, p=.025). Same trade-off as avg_content_word_len. Simple visceral language retains; sophisticated language hooks.

nodes.label: Exp × Sensory Mediation (NULL)
nodes.note: IMPORTANT: Sensory does NOT mediate exp→retention (only 8.7% mediated). Exp path c=0.326, c'=0.298 (barely changed). Sensory and experiential are INDEPENDENT channels to retention. Both should be maximized separately.

nodes.label: Negative Exp × Sensory (STRONGEST)
nodes.note: STRONGEST INTERACTION: negative exp words × sensory compound. r=+0.315 vs retention (p=5e-6). partial_r(dur)=+0.299, partial_r(exp)=+0.224. Describing danger/pain in concrete sensory terms = peak retention. ACTIONABLE: combine physical description with danger/discomfort narration.

nodes.label: Danger Words (best neg subtype)
nodes.note: dangerous/scary/terrified/deadly. r=+0.227 vs retention (p=.001). partial_r(exp)=+0.192. Strongest negative subtype. But low prevalence (n=7 videos).

nodes.label: Struggle Words (HURTS retention)
nodes.note: COUNTER-INTUITIVE: trying/barely/almost/struggled. r=-0.119 vs retention (p=.09). Struggle language HURTS. Describing difficulty ≠ describing danger/sensation. Show don't tell the struggle.

nodes.label: Valence (partially redundant)
nodes.note: VALIDATED BUT PARTIALLY REDUNDANT: Bootstrap CI [0.17,0.42]. Spearman 0.29. LOO [0.28,0.32]. BUT partial_r(dur+exp+sens)=0.094 (ns). Adds CV R²=+0.013 to model. 20-seed: 0.336±0.027. Not fully independent.

nodes.label: "Crazy" (top individual word)
nodes.note: Single most impactful word: 'crazy'. r=+0.208 vs retention (p=.003). Used in 37/202 videos (18%). Also: 'dangerous' r=+0.221, 'hurt' r=+0.145.

nodes.label: Consecutive I-Sentences (HURTS)
nodes.note: RETRACTED: r=-0.280 but only 9 videos have streak>=2, 2 have >=3. FALSE SIGNAL — driven by extreme outliers. Binary(>=3) r=-0.058 (ns). Does NOT add to model. Ignore.

nodes.label: Manner Adverbs (quickly/slowly)
nodes.note: quickly/slowly/carefully/barely per 100w. r=+0.157 vs keep (p=.026). Manner description adds engagement to hook.

nodes.label: Model v6 (with neg×sensory)
nodes.note: VALIDATED: dur+exp+pivot+sensory+neg_x_sensory. 20-seed CV R²=0.358 (+0.035 over base, 20/20 seeds positive). Bootstrap delta CI [0.001, 0.084]. Coefficients: dur=-0.207, exp=+1.302, pivot=+1.221, sensory=+0.680, neg_x_sens=+0.978. Q1→Q5: 73%→88% retention.

nodes.label: Tense Diversity (HURTS keep)
nodes.note: Number of tenses used (past/present/future). r=-0.229 vs keep (p=.001). Using fewer tense types = better keep. ACTIONABLE: stick to one or two tenses — don't switch between past/present/future.

nodes.label: Contrast Bigram Density
nodes.note: but+X/however+X/except+X bigram density. r=+0.185 vs keep (p=.008). Related to pivot density. Confirms pivots help keep at bigram level.

nodes.label: Setup/Anticipation Language
nodes.note: NEW: going to/gonna/let's see/about to/ready per 100w. r=+0.244 vs retention (p=.0005). partial_r(exp)=+0.217 — INDEPENDENT. partial_r(dur)=+0.158. ACTIONABLE: use setup/anticipation language to build forward momentum.

nodes.label: First-Person Immersion (I+sensory)
nodes.note: I/I'm within 5 words of experiential/sensory/physical words. r=+0.288 vs retention (p=3.3e-5). partial_r(exp)=+0.197 — partially independent. First-person immersive narration = peak retention.

nodes.label: Narrative Event Density
nodes.note: Common verbs per 100w. r=+0.150 vs retention (p=.033). More action events = more engagement.

nodes.label: Narrative Tension (neg+setup+pivot)
nodes.note: COMPOUND: negation + setup + pivot words per 100w. r=+0.265 vs retention (p=.0001). partial_r(exp)=+0.279 — INDEPENDENT. partial_r(dur)=+0.228. Combines 'can't/won't' + 'going to/let's see' + 'but/however'. ACTIONABLE: maximize narrative tension words.

nodes.label: Superlative Words (HURTS keep)
nodes.note: best/worst/most/biggest per 100w. r=-0.185 vs keep (p=.008). Extreme claims HURT keep. ACTIONABLE: avoid superlatives in script — show, don't claim.

nodes.label: Retention-Views Paradox
nodes.note: CRITICAL: Predicted retention quintiles Q1(73%)=10.4M views, Q5(88%)=4.9M views. High retention does NOT predict more views (0.46x ratio). Mega-viral driven by concept+keep, not retention optimization. Retention optimizes WITHIN concept tier.

nodes.label: Hook Word Uniqueness (HURTS keep)
nodes.note: Fraction of hook content words NOT repeated in body. r=-0.147 vs keep (p=.037). Unique hook concepts that aren't revisited = lower keep. Consistent with phrase_repetition helping. ACTIONABLE: callback to hook concepts in the body.

nodes.label: Transcript → Views (NULL)
nodes.note: CONFIRMED NULL: exp+sensory combined vs views r=0.067 (ns). pred_retention vs views r=-0.09 (ns). Transcript features do NOT meaningfully predict views directly. The causal chain goes features→keep/retention→views, and even that is noisy. Views driven by concept, timing, distribution.

nodes.label: Verb Diversity (HURTS keep)
nodes.note: Unique verbs / total verbs. r=-0.167 vs keep (p=.018). Using FEWER unique verbs (focused repetitive action) = better keep. Consistent with phrase_repetition and lexical_surprise findings.

nodes.label: Model Robustness Validation
nodes.note: ROBUST: 100-split leave-10%-out validation. Median R²=0.453, Mean R²=0.434. 100% positive splits. 5th pctl=0.125, 95th=0.706. Model generalizes: short(r=0.49), medium(r=0.55), long(r=0.73). Strongest for long videos with more transcript data.

nodes.label: Function/Content Word Ratio
nodes.note: r=+0.301 vs retention (p=1.4e-5). partial_r(exp)=+0.264 — INDEPENDENT. More conversational/simple language = better retention.

nodes.label: Unique Word Rate (HURTS keep)
nodes.note: r=-0.279 vs keep (p=5.9e-5). partial_r(dur)=-0.216. Unique vocabulary HURTS keep. ACTIONABLE: repeat key words.

nodes.label: Build Segments Below Baseline
nodes.note: BUILD keyword segments (so/then/next/started/using) are BELOW baseline (delta=-0.014, p=.001). PAYOFF segments ABOVE baseline (delta=+0.022, p=.027). But within-video: all segment types ns. Function labeling still mostly dead — only BUILD reliably predicts below-baseline.

nodes.label: Late Above-Baseline Fraction
nodes.note: BEST CURVE→VIEWS: Fraction of last 40% above average baseline. r=+0.345 vs views (p<.001). r=+0.818 vs retention. r=+0.276 vs keep. Staying above baseline late = most views. ab_area r=0.999 vs ret (tautological), ab_fraction r=0.296 vs views.

nodes.label: Pivot Words Slow Retention Decay
nodes.note: CAUSAL: Retention velocity at pivot words is LESS negative than non-pivot (-0.0068 vs -0.0095, p=2e-6, n=916). Pivots SLOW decay but don't reverse it. Pivots are brakes, not accelerators. ACTIONABLE: use pivots to protect retention at natural drop points.

nodes.label: Forward Causal Momentum (ep→next_ab)
nodes.note: FORWARD CAUSAL: Current segment exp-proc density predicts NEXT segment above-baseline r=+0.141 (p<.001, position-controlled). Exp: r=+0.102, proc: r=-0.097, sensory: r=+0.057. First evidence of forward-looking transcript→retention causation. ACTIONABLE: experiential content lifts NEXT section's retention.

nodes.label: Exp-Proc Trajectory Shape (NULL)
nodes.note: CONFIRMS placement_null: Trajectory SHAPE (slope, end-start, midpoint, variance) all ns after controlling for total level. partial(emp)=+0.07 max. Only total level matters, not trajectory shape.

nodes.label: Above-Baseline Window Enrichment
nodes.note: Above-baseline windows enriched: exp 1.76x (p<.001), neg_exp 2.61x (p<.001), shorter words (p<.001), higher unique_frac (p<.001). Depleted: proc 0.66x (p<.001). Confirms exp/proc/neg as retention drivers at window level.

nodes.label: Sensory Best at Mid-Video (Q3)
nodes.note: Sensory at Q3 (40-60%) has strongest retention correlation (r=+0.235). Q1=+0.14, Q2=0.00, Q3=+0.24, Q4=-0.02, Q5=+0.04. ACTIONABLE: pack sensory language in mid-video.

nodes.label: Negative Exp Best Early (Q1)
nodes.note: Negative exp in first 20% has strongest retention correlation (r=+0.217). Q1=+0.22, Q2=+0.12, Q3=+0.14, Q4=+0.05, Q5=+0.16. ACTIONABLE: establish danger/pain/stakes EARLY.

nodes.label: Danger→Sensory 1-2 Punch
nodes.note: DISCOVERY: Neg_exp segment followed by sensory segment = mean_ab=+0.075 (p=.002, n=14). 7x stronger than baseline (-0.001). Rare but powerful. ACTIONABLE: after danger/pain moment, immediately describe sensory detail. The 1-2 punch is the local mechanism for neg×sensory.

nodes.label: Proc Valley → Exp Recovery
nodes.note: After proc-heavy segments: next_exp predicts recovery (r=+0.09, p=.0006). Continuing proc = worst (r=-0.10, p=.0002). next_pivot also helps (r=+0.05, p=.04). ACTIONABLE: after building/explaining, switch to experiential or pivot to recover retention.

nodes.label: Sensory Words Accelerate Retention
nodes.note: ROLE CLARITY: Sensory words are the GAS PEDAL (accelerate retention, 2nd deriv r=+0.06, p=.004). Pivots are the BRAKE (slow decay r=-0.04, p=.001). Exp/neg_exp ns for acceleration. Different mechanisms: sensory creates forward momentum, pivots prevent loss.

nodes.label: Duration × Word-Type Interaction
nodes.note: CRITICAL INTERACTION: SHORT (<42s): sensory matters (r=+0.20). MEDIUM (43-50s): exp-proc strongest (r=+0.42). LONG (51+s): neg_exp dominates (r=+0.42), sensory null (r=+0.02). Recipe changes with duration. ACTIONABLE: short=describe, medium=feel, long=danger.

nodes.label: Elite Video Profile (updated)
nodes.note: Elite (n=21, top25% both, 6.5M vs 1.8M views): 2.17x neg_exp, 1.51x exp, 1.24x pivot. Longer sentences (184 vs 135). Above baseline at EVERY quintile (Q1=+0.074 to Q5=+0.103, always INCREASING). Elite sustain experiential with danger + pivots + flow.

nodes.label: Streak Monotonicity (one long > many short)
nodes.note: Max_streak/total_above r=+0.76 vs retention, r=+0.18 vs views. One LONG above-baseline streak >> many fragmented peaks. Sustained retention wins over scattered peaks. ACTIONABLE: build sustained runs, don't create peaks-and-valleys.

nodes.label: Common Exp Words Drive Signal
nodes.note: COMMON exp words (n=15, freq>=8: feel/crazy/hard/getting/break/surprised/strong/hurt/fast/hot) r=+0.27 vs retention. RARE exp words ns (r=+0.11). Not about novelty — about DENSITY of familiar visceral words.

nodes.label: Exp-Proc: ONLY Stable Signal
nodes.note: CRITICAL: 5-fold CV stability — exp-proc is the ONLY signal stable across all folds (all positive, mean r=+0.42±0.14). Sensory, neg_exp, pivot, vocab_div all UNSTABLE (some folds negative). Full model R²=0.371 (98% positive). emp IS the core signal.

nodes.label: Retention↔Keep Trade-Offs
nodes.note: ALIGNED (safe): emp (+ret +keep), pivot (+ret +keep). TRADE-OFF (risky): wps (+ret -keep), avg_word_len (-ret +keep), vocab_div (+ret -keep). SAFE TO MAXIMIZE: experiential-procedural and pivot density. Both help both metrics.

nodes.label: Builder Curve Shape (3.9x views)
nodes.note: Builder curves (n=93, 3.7M views) vs decayers (n=75, 947K views — 3.9x less). Only transcript predictor: vocab_diversity (lower = builder, p=.0002). All other features ns for shape. Shape mostly determined by non-transcript factors (concept, visual). ACTIONABLE: keep vocabulary focused/repetitive.

nodes.label: Short Video Recipe (<43s)
nodes.note: SHORT (<43s): pivot (r=+0.36 vs keep) and vocab_div (-0.30) dominate. No sig retention predictors. Composite r=+0.37 ret, r=+0.30 keep. Q1→Q5: 790K→2.5M views. RECIPE: more pivots, less diverse vocabulary.

nodes.label: Medium Video Recipe (43-51s)
nodes.note: MEDIUM (43-51s): emp dominates retention (r=+0.42). Composite r=+0.59 ret, r=+0.24 keep. Q1→Q5: 1.2M→5.3M views (4.3x). RECIPE: maximize experiential, minimize procedural, add pivots.

nodes.label: Long Video Recipe (>51s)
nodes.note: LONG (>51s): avg_word_len (r=-0.48), neg_exp (+0.42), emp (+0.41). Short words + danger = retention. Composite r=+0.57 ret but NEGATIVE keep. RECIPE: short visceral words, danger language, experiential. Warning: hurts keep.
